Retinal Occlusion Treatment – Vitreo Eye Clinic in Kochi

With 8 full-time consultants, this department offers the complete gamut of medical and surgical treatment for all diseases pertaining to the retina. All types of surgical procedures in the retina including managing complicated retinal detachments, advanced diabetic eye disease and injury to the retina following trauma and road accidents are performed regularly. The Department has one of the most advanced Macula services in the State of Kerala and has a large referral base from all Districts in Kerala.

Comprehensive Diabetic Retinopathy Care

The Institute offers the entire range of facilities for the diagnosis and treatment of Diabetic Retinopathy. The department conducts regular patient education programs and screening programs for patients with diabetes mellitus for early detection of diabetic retinopathy. The center is a tertiary referral center for the management of advanced diabetic retinopathy.

Retinopathy of Prematurity (ROP)

This condition which affects premature infants immediately after birth could be a blinding disease if not detected or left untreated at an early stage. Ophthalmologists trained in the treatment of ROP examine the eyes of premature infants in various neonatal centers in hospitals across the city and also in neighbouring districts for early detection of this condition. The Institute provides laser treatment for this particular condition and also surgical expertise for advanced cases.

What is Diabetic Retinopathy?

Diabetic Retinopathy is a serious eye condition caused by long-term diabetes. It affects the retina, the light-sensitive tissue at the back of the eye, and is a leading cause of vision loss and blindness among working-age adults. This condition results from damage to the tiny blood vessels in the retina due to prolonged high blood sugar levels.

Common Symptoms of Diabetic Retinopathy

In its early stages, diabetic retinopathy often has no symptoms. As it progresses, signs may include:

  • Blurred or fluctuating vision
  • Dark spots or floaters in your vision
  • Impaired color perception
  • Partial or total vision loss

Causes and Risk Factors

Diabetic retinopathy is primarily caused by uncontrolled diabetes, but several other factors can increase the risk:

  • Long duration of diabetes
  • Poor blood sugar control
  • High blood pressure (hypertension)
  • Elevated cholesterol levels
  • Smoking
  • Pregnancy

Diagnosis of Diabetic Retinopathy

Early diagnosis is key to preventing vision loss. Common diagnostic tests include:

  • Dilated fundus examination by an ophthalmologist
  • Fundus photography to capture images of the retina
  • Optical Coherence Tomography (OCT) for detailed retinal imaging
  • Fluorescein angiography to check blood vessel leakage

Stages of Diabetic Retinopathy

Non-Proliferative Diabetic Retinopathy (NPDR) – Early stage with microaneurysms and small hemorrhages.

Proliferative Diabetic Retinopathy (PDR) – Advanced stage with abnormal blood vessel growth, leading to higher risk of retinal detachment and vitreous hemorrhage.

Treatment Options for Diabetic Retinopathy

Treatment depends on the stage and severity of the disease:

  • Blood sugar, blood pressure, and cholesterol control (early stages)
  • Laser photocoagulation to seal leaking blood vessels
  • Anti-VEGF injections to stop abnormal vessel growth
  • Steroid injections to reduce inflammation
  • Vitrectomy surgery for advanced cases

Prevention and Regular Screening

Prevention of diabetic retinopathy starts with managing diabetes effectively:

  • Keep blood sugar, blood pressure, and cholesterol levels within the recommended range
  • Quit smoking
  • Undergo annual dilated eye exams, even if no symptoms are present

Why Regular Eye Exams Matter

Diabetic retinopathy can progress without warning signs. Regular eye check-ups help detect changes early and prevent irreversible vision loss.

Get Expert Care for Diabetic Retinopathy

If you have diabetes, consult an eye specialist regularly to monitor and manage your eye health. Early diagnosis and timely treatment can preserve vision and improve quality of life.

  • Know About Diabetic Retinopathy

  • Did You Know?

  • Why Giridhar Hospital?

  • Any patient with diabetes mellitus should undergo a retinal examination at the time of diagnosis.

  • Once early retinopathy is detected, regular examinations at least once a year are necessary.

  • Early retinopathy can only be detected through periodic retinal examinations.

  • Good control of hypertension in patients with diabetes reduces the incidence of retinopathy by 37%.

  • Timely treatment of diabetic retinopathy can prevent significant vision loss.

  • You can have advanced retinopathy despite having good vision.

  • Vision loss can be sudden or rapid due to bleeding inside the eye.

  • If diabetic retinopathy is neglected, it can lead to blindness.

  • Approximately 1 in 5 adults in India has diabetes.
  • Approximately 20% of people with diabetes have diabetic retinopathy.
  • Diabetic retinopathy is a silent disease, and symptoms usually appear only in the later stages.
  • 1 in 10 people with diabetic retinopathy have vision-threatening retinopathy, which can be detected through screening eye examinations.
  • Ideally, every person with diabetic retinopathy should undergo a dilated retinal examination once every year.
  • Diabetic retinopathy is an important VISION-THREATENING DISEASE.

Frequently Asked Questions

Explore our frequently asked questions about vitreo retina to gain insights into conditions, treatments, and preventive care for optimal eye health.

What symptoms might indicate a retinal problem?

Common symptoms include sudden vision loss, floaters, flashes of light, and distorted vision. If experienced, seek immediate medical attention.

How is diabetic retinopathy treated?

Treatment options include laser therapy, injections of anti-VEGF medications, and vitrectomy for advanced cases, tailored to individual needs.

What role does a vitreo-retinal specialist play?

A vitreo-retinal specialist diagnoses and treats conditions affecting the retina and vitreous, using advanced techniques to preserve vision.

Can retinal detachment be repaired?

Yes, retinal detachment can often be repaired through surgical procedures such as pneumatic retinopexy, scleral buckle, or vitrectomy.
What are the risk factors for retinal diseases?
Key risk factors include diabetes, high blood pressure, family history, aging, and previous eye injuries. Regular screenings are essential for early detection.
How often should I have retinal screenings?
It is recommended to have a comprehensive eye exam every one to two years, or more frequently if you have risk factors.
What can I expect during a vitreo-retinal examination?
Expect dilated eye exams to assess the retina’s health. The process may involve imaging tests such as OCT or fluorescein angiography.
Can lifestyle changes support retinal health?
Yes, maintaining a healthy diet rich in antioxidants, controlling blood sugar levels, avoiding smoking, and managing blood pressure can promote retinal health.
